2022-12-29, 10:35 p.m. - Arif Rabbani Russel: ****

Guys,

Regarding our AGM and elections- there are few points I  want to point out:


1. Most of us were engaged with the football World Cup until 3rd week of December. 

2. After that people are mostly busy with vacations or f&f gatherings. 

3. The most important one: we don’t know whether we will have a 4th team or not! If yes, we need to restructure the selection procedure/committee.

4. Leadership change (myself, overall executive committee, captains, split captaincy etc) - we should have a constructive decision among ourselves. 

Doing all of these in the last week of December seemed too rush.

So let’s have our discussion on restructuring, elections and AGM in late January or February.

With our practice starting in January 8th, our members will be engaged with cricket again and be aware of the changes and benefits. 

Also 2022 leadership started in February/March - so not delayed that way. 

Will see you all soon.

All the clubs are kindly requested to choose one of these three options
by FEB 07, 2023 to be applicable from this season onwards. We will go
with the majority and the choices all the clubs will make will be
transparent to everyone.

Option 1: No changes at all in terms of the balls,
$300 increase in T40/50 overs team registration and
$300 increase in T20 overs team registration.

Option 2: White ball change from Duke to Kookaburra Senator for T40/50
overs format,
$700 increase in T40/50 overs team registration and
$300 increase in T20 overs team registration.

Option 3: 2 White ball change from Duke to Kookaburra Senator for T40/50
overs format and 2 extra games for T40/50 over format means 14 games
instead of 12 games per T40/50 overs format team,
$1000 increase in T40/50 overs team registration and
$300 increase in T20 overs team registration.


Thank you,
E&DCL Executives
EDMONTON & DISTRICT CRICKET LEAGUE
www.cricketedmonton.com

2023-02-09, 6:22 p.m. - +1 (780) 803-9806: 2 to 5 minutes boktita ditey chaai, khabar khawar por.
2023-02-09, 6:23 p.m. - JD: Long text coming up. I just had an aha moment today that I want to share. It should be beneficial for everyone. (those of you who want to improve your cricket)
2023-02-09, 6:24 p.m. - JD: I just had my aha moment today, I have been playing soccer for the last week or so, and I was playing with regular running shoes/gym shoes or whatever you want to call it. But I could not keep up, felt like I was slipping all the time; I could not switch and turn in time, could not gain speed and stop at the right moment; I was playing poorly, but in my mind, I was a better player than that.
2023-02-09, 6:24 p.m. - JD: Everything changed ever since I got indoor soccer shoes. The game just improved tenfold, and today, I just realized that some of our top players were also struggling even though they had indoor soccer cleats. Well, the problem was that we were playing on the gym floor and cleats are meant to be for Turf.
2023-02-09, 6:26 p.m. - JD: Long story short, I think we can improve our cricket by alot by investing in the right shoes for the right track. During my aha moment, I was having flashbacks of how many times I lost my wicket because I couldn't get a proper grip when I played a shot. It happens to all of us, missed catches and fielding because we lost our footing. The funny thing is this shit happens while playing with cricket shoes. It looks super silly from outside way to explain the phenomenon. Sure, all of us can relate to it. Cricket shoes are junk, not made for the fields we play on. They are made for your nice tailored professional flat ground. Last year, I saw a couple guys fielding with outdoor soccer cleats (wouldn't be a bad idea to give it a try)
2023-02-09, 6:29 p.m. - JD: I'll discuss this in detail when we meet up. Something to think about. So I don't forget about it.

Invest in shoes, not freaking new bats. 

We should not be batting with the same shoes on Victoria and Millwood pitch. Even our bowlers would be able to bowl a lot quicker if they had the right shoes for the suitable surface. That would also explain why our bowlers seem to ball quicker during indoor practice.

Should write a thesis on this…
2023-02-09, 6:38 p.m. - Mousum: Totally agree
2yrs back I bought spike shoes (detachable) from Govind and that was a proper cricket shoe. That year EDCL changed the rule and forced not to use spikes while batting (to most extend while bowling as well).

2023-02-13, 9:32 a.m. - Simon Bhai Tanveer Ahmed: FYI: We forgot to announce during AGM:

According to RBCC bylaws, people have to spend a season before they can vote or run for a position. That is done for 2 reasons I believe: 

1. When they vote, they did not get chance to meet the nembers. So their vote maybe more on appearance rather than informed.

2. When they run, voters do not know him. So a good candidate may loose bcz he is not known, and eventually it quashes their enthusiasm.

I apologize, the thing was not mentioned in AGM.

*Topic: Payment of membership fees*
*Deadline: March 11, 2023*


Hello RBCC members, 

I would like to request all members to e-transfer their membership fees by March 11, 2023 to  rbcc.executives@gmail.com.

- The deadline for EDCL registration fees ($7500 and additional ~ $2400 for Team B T20 registration) is March 12, 2023, so we need to collect the membership fees before that.

- The membership fee is $300 for regular members and $150 for those students who do not have funding (such as undergraduate students).

- If you are able to pay the full membership fees, (or any amount greater than the minimum amount of $50), please do so. It will be much appreciated as we have large upcoming expenses (EDCL registration, Kinsmen booking etc.)

- Each member is expected to send at least the min amount of $50 by March 11th. Please be informed that it is critical to pay this amount in order to be considered for the games.

- If (for any reason) you are unable to pay the minimum amount, please establish a communication with an Exec member before March 11th. 

- Lastly, thank you all. If you have any questions/concerns feel free to message me directly

Topic: Team A Captaincy

Hi everyone, unfortunately our elected Team A Captain Adnan Kamal is unable to carry on with Captaincy duties this season due to personal reasons and has stepped down as Team A Captain, effective immediately. Adnan bhai will still be fully committed to play the season as an RBCC player in both formats.

We would like to  request club members to respect personal boundaries as well and be accommodating to his  decision as a club and his team mates. 

At this time, the Execs are working on   procedures of electing a new Captain following club protocols and would like everyone to stay tuned for upcoming announcements regarding this matter.

Thank you, 
VP Comm

2023-04-03, 9:59 a.m. - Wasel Ur Rahman: 📣📣📣ANNOUNCEMENT📣📣📣📣

Topic: *Invitation for Team A Captaincy Nomination* *(Revised)*

Hi everyone, on next step on assigning a Team A Captain, we Execs would like to call for nominations for the position *RBCC Team A Captain*. This is a *REVISED* version of the original post, so please review carefully.

As per club by laws, nominations can be submitted from any RBCC members given: 

1) member has been with a club for at least one season

2) nomination is submitted by the individual themselves 

3) is able and available to carry out Team A Captaincy throughout the 2023 season.

*For current leadership team (IF Applicable):*

1) Any individual currently assigned to a leadership or Exec position but interested to submit a nomination for Team A Captaincy should  submit nomination right away. However, members are requested to hold on to the position until to nominations withdrawal period is completed.

2) Once nomination withdrawal is completed, the member will need to surrender their current Exec/Leadership position prior to running for election. 

3) Once a position is surrendered, regardless of the outcome of Team A Captaincy election, the member cannot return to the original position. A general election will be held to fill the position and the member can run again if they wish to.

*DEADLINE* for submitting nominations is *Tuesday, April 4th, 11:59 pm* . Please submit the nominations by directly contacting VP Comm (Wasel) to keep consistency in communication.

Please note, if an election is required based on nominations, it will be conducted by the current Execs to expedite the process and results will be shared with full transparency to the rest of RBCC members. If further clarification is required or any questions arise, please don't hesitate to contact me or other Execs. 

- Thank you
VP Comm

Hi everyone, with the withdrawal deadline closing last night, it is time to decide on RBCC Team A Captain. Election or no election? You will have to wait till the end of the post :). As Pritam currently holds the VP Admin position, we would like him to surrender the position before accepting the Team A Captain role. More details to backfill VP Admin are to be shared with the group. Thank you everyone, and have a great day.

On behalf of Execs

2023-04-08, 1:00 p.m. - Simon Bhai Tanveer Ahmed: Practice does not make perfect. Practice makes permanent 

You don’t get better from repetition. This is an outdated model. Sport has too many variables for it to be about repeating the same delivery all the time

1 degree of difference in release position leads to 1 foot of difference at the batters end. No delivery will ever be exactly the same. Whether neural fatigue, injury, cognitive stress or physical stress. So why practice with the mindset that it’s about repeating the same thing? Why do we think that everything is always the same?

So what is the answer ? 

Train with variability . Train to try and come up with solutions. Train for the perturbations that are guaranteed to happen in sport. 

Steffan Jones
Rajasthan Royals HP Fast bowling coach

*Topic: Membership Dues and Extra Jersey Payment Request*

Dear RBCC members,

I would like to update you on our current financial situation and request your support. As you may be aware, we have recently received the final invoice from EDCL, which includes the additional registration and season dues for our 4th Div T20 team, bringing the total invoice amount to $10,800. Unfortunately, we still owe $3900 to EDCL and the payment is due by April 16th, 2023. Additionally, we have expenses coming up for the Kinsmen Indoor Practice sessions, and our bank balance is running low.

*Membership Dues Payment:*
I kindly request that members pay the full amount owed if possible or contribute any amount you can at this time. Since our primary source of revenue from the circus will not be available to us until June, we are in urgent need of your support in paying the remaining $3900 to EDCL by the deadline and covering the upcoming expenses for the indoor practice.

*Extra Jersey Payment:*
We will be holding a jersey distribution event, similar to last year. However, to help alleviate our current financial situation, I would like to begin collecting payments in advance. I will reach out to the members individually to inform them of the amount owed for any extra jerseys ordered. ($30 per jersey and $15 per F&F T-shirt)

You can send e-transfer to rbcc.executives@gmail.com . 
Thank you for your understanding and cooperation in helping us maintain a healthy financial balance. We appreciate your continued support of RBCC, and we look forward to a successful season ahead. 

Best regards,
Nitun

2023-04-16, 4:23 p.m. - Nitun Kundu: Dear members,
 
This is a solid warning for those using the MILLWOODS ground during the beginning of spring when the grass is sprouting. This is a clear violation of the home base ground agreement and must stop immediately.
 
The sports field is a precious resource that we all share, and it requires proper care and maintenance to ensure its longevity. Sprouting grass during the beginning of spring is a critical time for the field's growth and recovery after winter. Continued use of the field during this time can cause irreparable damage to the delicate grass and disrupt the growing process.
 
We urge all EDCL members to comply with the home base ground agreement we have with the City of Edmonton, which prohibits the use of the field during the sprouting period. Violators will face strict consequences, including possible suspension or loss of access to the field.
From now on and as per previous correspondence, "The Use of EDCL Grounds and Practice Nets" are mandatory and must be abided by at all times to keep your membership in good standing with the organization.
 
From now on, if you notice anyone defies this standing order, please send evidence(s) (Picture/Video) when you file a complaint to the secretary. It can help the executive take solid disciplinary action against the offenders. Your cooperation in the matter is greatly appreciated.
 
Please respect the rules and regulations in place to protect our sports field and allow it to thrive. Your cooperation is crucial in maintaining a safe and healthy playing surface for everyone to enjoy. Let's work together to ensure the longevity of our sports field.
Thank you for your attention and understanding.
- EDCL

2023-04-17, 5:41 p.m. - Wasel Ur Rahman: 📣📣📣ANNOUNCEMENT📣📣📣📣

Topic: *Invitation for VP Admin Nomination* 

Hi everyone, the Exec  Team is looking to fill back the position for VP Admin and are inviting nominations. 

As per club by laws, nominations can be submitted from any RBCC members given: 

1) member has been with a club for at least one season

2) nomination is submitted by the individual themselves 

3) is able and available to carry out VP Admin position throughout the 2023 season.

*For current leadership team (IF Applicable):*

1) Any individual currently assigned to a leadership or Exec position but interested to submit a nomination for VP Admin should  submit nomination right away. However, members are requested to hold on to the position until to nominations withdrawal period is completed.

2) Once nomination withdrawal is completed, the member will need to surrender their current Exec/Leadership position prior to running for election. 

3) Once a position is surrendered, regardless of the outcome of VP Admin election, the member cannot return to the original position. A general election will be held to fill the position and the member can run again if they wish to.

*DEADLINE* for submitting nominations is *Wednesday, April 19th, 10:00 pm* . Please submit the nominations by directly contacting VP Comm (Wasel) to keep consistency in communication.

Please note, if an election is required based on nominations, it will be conducted by the current Execs to expedite the process and results will be shared with full transparency to the rest of RBCC members. If further clarification is required or any questions arise, please don't hesitate to contact me or other Execs. 

- Thank you
VP Comm

As announced in SAGM, we are delighted to introduce CricHeroes as EDCL's digital partner for a one-stop shop for all your digital needs.....

Our EDCL app is ready; please ask your team members to download the app, sign in, and complete the 'player registration' portal to complete the profile before April 25, 2023. Alberta Health Card - (If someone doesn't have the physical card - the number should be written down, and a self-attested copy MUST be uploaded. 
An ID-style picture/portrait selfie (No fancy pictures) 
Role of the Club Admin

2023-04-22, 11:07 a.m. - sakiB: One can use existing profile in CricClubs to register in the new app, that doesn’t require to provide IDs. In CricClubs, going through “forgot your password” password can be updated. It will send OTP to the cell number in that CricClubs profile.

Once password updated in  CricClubs, sign in with the same email address in the new App. In the new app OTP is sent to email address first, then to phone number. That should transfer all the profile information/stats into the new App from CricClubs profile.

2023-04-27, 11:29 p.m. - Simon Bhai Tanveer Ahmed: Hello all, 
Canadian national coach, Mr. Pubudu Dassanayake, will be touring our city as part of the 'Canada Calling' campaign to recruit local talents to the national camp. This is an excellent opportunity for aspiring cricketers in our town to showcase their talent and potentially make it to the national level.


The EDCL selectors will be shortlisting the local contenders for the upcoming trials. The national coach and ACA selectors will closely observe the participants' performances and select the most promising athletes for further training and development.

As one of the major leagues in Western Canada, EDCL is excited about this event, and we are sure that many of you are also looking forward to it. If you haven't been part of EDCL yet, don't get disappointed; you can email edcl@cricketedmonton.com to register for the trials. 

The event is scheduled for MAY 1, 2023, at Millwoods Ground. Please spread the word and encourage all the potential players in our town to participate in this event. Let us all come together to support and promote our local talent and help them achieve their dreams.

Wishing good luck to all contenders!


Executives.

Topic: Team B Captaincy

Hi everyone, unfortunately our elected Team B Captain Yaseen Biplob Sheikh has resigned from his Captaincy duties this season due to family commitments and personal reasons, effective immediately. Biplob bhai is not yet sure if he can fully stay committed throughout the season as an RBCC player in both formats and believes the Captaincy duties will be hard for him to carry on.

Although we regret to see this change, but RBCC Execs would like to recognize personal boundaries within the club and wish Biplob bhai all the best with his family and cricket life. We would like to  request club members to respect personal boundaries as well and be accommodating to his  decision as a club and his team mates. 

At this time, the Execs are working on   procedures of electing a new Captain following club protocols and would like everyone to stay tuned for upcoming announcements regarding this matter.

Thank you, 
VP Comm

2023-04-30, 3:47 p.m. - Wasel Ur Rahman: 📣📣📣ANNOUNCEMENT📣📣📣📣

Topic: *Invitation for VP Admin Nomination* 

Hi everyone, the Exec  Team is looking to fill back the position for VP Admin and are inviting nominations. We haven't received nominations in the last call so asking members to reconsider the invite.

As per club by laws, nominations can be submitted from any RBCC members given: 

1) member has been with a club for at least one season

2) nomination is submitted by the individual themselves 

3) is able and available to carry out VP Admin position throughout the 2023 season.

*For current leadership team (IF Applicable):*

1) Any individual currently assigned to a leadership or Exec position but interested to submit a nomination for VP Admin should  submit nomination right away. However, members are requested to hold on to the position until to nominations withdrawal period is completed.

2) Once nomination withdrawal is completed, the member will need to surrender their current Exec/Leadership position prior to running for election. 

3) Once a position is surrendered, regardless of the outcome of VP Admin election, the member cannot return to the original position. A general election will be held to fill the position and the member can run again if they wish to.

*DEADLINE* for submitting nominations is *Monday, May 1st 10:00 pm* . 
Please submit the nominations by directly contacting VP Comm (Wasel) to keep consistency in communication.

Please note, if an election is required based on nominations, it will be conducted by the current Execs to expedite the process and results will be shared with full transparency to the rest of RBCC members. If further clarification is required or any questions arise, please don't hesitate to contact me or other Execs. 

- Thank you
VP Comm

2023-05-03, 12:52 p.m. - Nitun Kundu: 📣📣📣📣*ANNOUNCEMENT*📣📣📣📣

*Topic: RBCC Membership Fees - Important Information for New Members*

Greetings RBCC members,

This message is primarily intended for our new players who recently joined RBCC and registered in the EDCL app. First and foremost, welcome to RBCC! We are thrilled to have you and look forward to an exciting season together. I would like to take this opportunity to provide some important information about the membership fees.

- Regular members are required to pay a membership fee of $300, while students who do not have funding, such as undergraduate students, are required to pay a fee of $150. You can send the membership fee via e-transfer to rbcc.executives@gmail.com.

- To be considered for the games, each member is expected to send at least the minimum amount of $50 at this point. If you are unable to pay the minimum amount right now, we request you to get in touch with an Exec member at your earliest convenience.

- If possible, we kindly request that you pay the full membership fee, or any amount greater than $50, as we have significant upcoming expenses. We greatly appreciate your support in this matter.

- Lastly, if you have any questions or concerns, feel free to message me directly.

Thank you,
-Nitun

Topic: *Invitation for Team B Vice Captaincy Nomination* 
*DEADLINE- Tuesday, May 9th, 12:00 PM (Noon)*

Hi everyone, as Sharif Bhai accepted the captaincy of Team B, it left the position of Team B Vice Captain vacant. Per next step of fulfilling this significant role in Team B, we Execs would like to call for nominations for the position *RBCC Team B Vice Captain*.

*DEADLINE* for submitting nominations is *Tuesday, May 9th, 12:00 PM (Noon)* . Please submit the nominations by directly contacting me, VP Admin (Rhittiq), to keep consistency in communication.

As per club by laws, nominations can be submitted from any RBCC members given: 

1) member has been with a club for at least one season

2) nomination is submitted by the individual themselves 

3) is able and available to carry out Team B Vice Captaincy throughout the 2023 season.

*For current leadership team (IF Applicable):*

1) Any individual currently assigned to a leadership or Exec position but interested to submit a nomination for Team B Vice Captaincy should  submit nomination right away. However, members are requested to hold on to the position until to nominations withdrawal period is completed.

2) Once nomination withdrawal is completed, the member will need to surrender their current Exec/Leadership position prior to running for election. 

3) Once a position is surrendered, regardless of the outcome of Team B Vice Captaincy election, the member cannot return to the original position. A general election will be held to fill the position and the member can run again if they wish to.

Please note, if an election is required based on nominations, it will be conducted by the current Execs to expedite the process and results will be shared with full transparency to the rest of RBCC members. If further clarification is required or any questions arise, please don't hesitate to contact me or other Execs. 

- Thank you
VP Admin

2023-05-12, 11:16 a.m. - Sharif Bhai: *My pledge as a RBB Captain*

I will try to create a positive and inclusive environment where every member feels valued and supported. *To achieve that we all need to sacrifice a little*. I believe little sacrifices from everyone is necessary for “the greatest  amount of good for the greatest number of people” (if you know you you know which philosophical creed I like 😉). 

By working together as a team and building strong relationships, we can achieve our goals and enjoy the sport of cricket to the fullest. 

Game performance is certainly important but I will personally place high value on team/ club engagement. 

In my view primary objectives of RBB are:

1. To offer opportunities for all members to experience and enjoy playing cricket.

2. To foster the development of new players, giving a stable environment to improve their skills. 

*Please make my word and hold me accountable*

2023-05-12, 11:39 p.m. - Asif (Rittik) Chowdhury: Hello Teams,

We are writing to inform you about a significant update to the regulations regarding footwear on the EDCL grounds, specifically in relation to Victoria Park (VP) wicket.

After careful consideration and consultation with relevant authorities, it has been decided that using metal spikes will be strictly prohibited on the VP, effective immediately.


Metal spikes, while offering excellent traction, have been identified as a potential risk factor that can cause damage to the turf, resulting in relentless repairs and compromised playing conditions.


We recommend that players switch to appropriate footwear options that provide sufficient grip without the use of metal spikes. Rubber-soled shoes or spikes made of synthetic materials are viable alternatives that offer stability and traction while minimizing potential damage to the wicket.


Please circulate this information to all your club members to ensure their awareness and compliance with this new regulation. It is vital that everyone involved in the game understands the rationale behind this decision and cooperates fully.

From EDCL

2023-05-15, 8:00 p.m. - Sharif Bhai: I would like to clarify that the selection of the B team is influenced by numerous factors. These include the duration of membership, team engagement, team chemistry, and playing roles. For instance, if we were to include more than 5 bowlers in the team, we would not be able to allocate them their full quota of overs. 

If you are not selected at this time, please understand that it is not a reflection on your abilities, but rather a result of these selection factors. However, if you are available for all the games, you can expect to participate in at least three - five matches.

Hence, I urge you to actively involve yourself with the Club and consistently follow a practice routine. This will ensure that you are well-prepared to make a significant impact when your chance to shine presents itself.

2023-06-02, 9:09 a.m. - JD: 📣📣📣 📣ANNOUNCEMENT📣📣📣📣

Topic: Volunteer Signup for Royal Canadian Circus 
 
Link: https://forms.gle/epPuSVp75QvvEBdN8

Hello RBCC members,

June is upon us. It's a big time for RBCC both on and off the field.

June 8-18, we are volunteering in circus. It is a huge time commitment, which brings the much needed money to manage the club. And to do it successfully, we need ALL hands on deck.

Please sign up for 6 shifts minimum, as agreed during AGM. If everyone covers 6 shift, we get 75-80% of shifts covered.

But there is still leftover 20% can’t left undone. We need support from your friends and family for that. Please encourage them to volunteer with us too. It helps the club, allow them the watch the circus freely, and also give them volunteer experience usable in CV.

Let's finish this feat and carry on with the great season we are having. By the end of the season, we wish to have enough money to celebrate the trophies we are winning this year... 

Some of the hardest shifts to cover for all of us are the Friday (3-6:30 shifts), we need your cooperation. 

Thank you,
On Behalf of Execs & Circus committee
2023-06-02, 9:14 a.m. - Sudeepta: I'll come after work if people are still there
2023-06-02, 9:29 a.m. - Pritam Da: Everyone, please try to do as many shifts as possible. Ask your FnF to join you as well. They just need to sit close to one of the gates, and during the intermission, just stand on the exit door and advise people to use the main entrance, and enjoy the show in the process.

This is tremendously important for the sustainability of our club by ourselves. 

It also reduces team dues, gets more practice sessions, and assists the club in organizing more events.

2023-06-05, 11:33 a.m. - Razib Bhai: 📣📣📣 📣ANNOUNCEMENT📣📣📣📣

Topic: Volunteer Signup for Royal Canadian Circus 
 
Link: https://forms.gle/epPuSVp75QvvEBdN8

Hello RBCC members,

June is upon us. It's a big time for RBCC both on and off the field.

June 8-18, we are volunteering in circus. It is a huge time commitment, which brings the much needed money to manage the club. And to do it successfully, we need ALL hands on deck.

Please sign up for 6 shifts minimum, as agreed during AGM. If everyone covers 6 shift, we get 75-80% of shifts covered.

But there is still leftover 20% can’t left undone. We need support from your friends and family for that. Please encourage them to volunteer with us too. It helps the club, allow them the watch the circus freely, and also give them volunteer experience usable in CV.

Let's finish this feat and carry on with the great season we are having. By the end of the season, we wish to have enough money to celebrate the trophies we are winning this year... 

Some of the hardest shifts to cover for all of us are the Friday (3-6:30 shifts), we need your cooperation. 

Thank you,
On Behalf of Execs & Circus committee

2023-06-30, 1:22 p.m. - Pritam Da: Simon's Message regarding BCAE Membership :


Dear leaders,

During the BSAUA vs BCAE match, I was a proud member of BSAUA. But then somehow I realized that I have a great distance from BCAE, from where, RBCC has got a lot of support previously. And honestly, there have been a lot of gaps and misunderstandings solely created by this distance. So, I just applied for BCAE membership right before the match, to reflect that there is no personal agenda against BCAE with me. 

To be honest, I think more people should be involved with both BSAUA and BCAE at this point. These are the other two flagbearers of Bangladesh like us, and we need their support from many aspects. Particularly, if we want RBCC to grow, if we want our kids to learn cricket and have a next generation, we need a good support system, and being affiliated with them will help us in that cause. There are so many great souls who love us and follow us, but feels as if there is a pushback from our side, which we should eliminate. So, I think, if possible, more people should join and actively engage with these organizations, especially BCAE (as you won't have to register for a course for that).
2023-06-30, 1:25 p.m. - Susen Das: So, membership for family is only $10 as a RBCC player?
2023-06-30, 1:26 p.m. - Susen Das: Cause $20 will be covered by sponsors? Any idea about these sponsors?
2023-06-30, 1:28 p.m. - Sharif Bhai: Here you go!!! Joined 🤓
2023-06-30, 1:31 p.m. - Tanvir: Still probably wont get it if its even free
2023-06-30, 1:55 p.m. - Sharif Bhai: To advance it’s really beneficial to create collaborative environment. We definitely can survive without others but what harm can be caused if we have better connections with other organizations.
2023-06-30, 4:02 p.m. - Simon Bhai Tanveer Ahmed: You will be surprised
2023-06-30, 4:08 p.m. - Simon Bhai Tanveer Ahmed: We want to grow RBCC as a platform for cricket and sports for Bangladeshi and close community in Edmonton. Also, as a club, we need more support and involvement, cheer and passion coming from our community. We want RBCC to be felt and loved by every cricket fan.

To do that:
1. We need to perform well. Sudeepta has taken the lead to help us along with our senior cricketers in that aspect.

2. We need to outreach and involve more with community. Our exectuive and leadership team is working on that.

In a few season, we want to see that an RBCC game is filled with spectators, supproting us, cheering for us. If we want to go to that level, we need to take a couple steps- step 1 and step 2.

Being a member of our community groups and actively participating with them is one stretch for step 2. Coming to practice is the stretch to step 1.

*Topic: RBCC Mid Season Feedback*

*DEADLINE: Thursday, July 20, 11:59 pm* 

Dear RBCC members,

We are roughly midway through the 2023 season for both Team A and B. At this time we would like to invite feedback from all RBCC members regarding the season so far. Any concerns, questions, or new ideas that can help the remainder of the season and/or the club move forward is most welcome.

Given the busy schedule this summer, it would be most efficient if the RBCC members can provide their feedback by filling out the Google form in the link below: 

https://forms.gle/u38uPznpZsxofSLe7

*Your responses will be visible to the Execs only.*

Responses will not be shared to any club members, Captains/VCs, or Leadership team and will be solely used for the purposes of betterment of the club.

*DEADLINE: Thursday, July 20, 11:59 pm* 

We highly encourage all members to participate as it will help RBCC to be comprehensive and inclusive in making future decisions.

If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to reach out to anyone of the Execs. 

Regards,
VP Comm

2023-08-10, 6:35 p.m. - Russell: T20 restriction list is the list that was provided before the start of the season. The list doesn’t change. The restricted 11 cannot play for the A team for this season.
2023-08-10, 6:40 p.m. - Sudeepta: why does the 40 over list change and not the t20?
2023-08-10, 6:53 p.m. - Russell: Because in 40/50 overs, 7 players are restricted. But in T20s, 11. That’s one of the reasons
2023-08-10, 7:16 p.m. - Pritam Da: Big Clubs like Challengers, Gujarat, Punjab, Kings, Shakti, Ginny etc have multiple teams & they restricted any 11 of their choice & some of them probably dont even play. Hence, they can play young good players in their A/B/C T20 teams. 

Mid Season restricted list gets updated based on stats & that will restrict a lot of these strong performers which will restrict their chances to qualify for playoffs, especially in a ran affected season like this. Hence T20 list is not been released as members of these clubs mainly have executive positions as well in EDCL.

Moving Forward, we need to keep this in mind while providing our own restrictions list.

2023-08-27, 1:56 p.m. - Pritam Da: The main reason Most of us cant throw is probably the rotator calf muscle injury. Its a very tiny muscle behind the shoulder blades which we almost never use in our everyday life, hence it remains inactive & gets weaker & weaker over time.

It becomes weak over time & causes pain in our biceps/triceps area when we throw.

Whenever I did the rehab for at least 3 weeks, I felt I could throw slightly better.

However,  the proper rehab may take upto 3/4 months & because of our age, we need to do keep on doing those exercises regularly to ensure that rotator calf remains active & strong.

2023-10-04, 2:57 p.m. - Sharif Bhai: *Bangladesh Consular Service in Edmonton - Registration Link*

Respected Community Members,

Another great news to share! Bangladesh Consular Service coming to Edmonton!!

BCAE is excited to announce that Bangladesh High Commission Ottawa started registration for Bangladesh Consular Service in Edmonton. Please use the link for registration:  https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdJrdtsVdAsfr7cOxqM9Y3aNMKM2KJ6dJH57d1JqvE6ctYOTw/viewform  

Note:
Consular service will be provided based on the number of registrations.
The Venue and Dates of the service will be communicated later through the High Commission’s Website and Facebook

With Best Regards,

Syed Tauhid Zuhori, PhD (He/Him)

On behalf of BCAE Executive Committee 2023

This season has been filled with remarkable achievements, both as teams and individuals. Beyond that, it's been a fantastic collective effort to navigate through the entire season, just like every season before.

2024-01-27, 11:23 p.m. - Susen Das: Read his story here:

ছবির মানুষটির নাম শামার জোসেফ। টেস্ট ক্যারিয়ারের প্রথম বলেই উইকেট শিকার করেছেন আজকে। সেটিও স্টিভেন স্মিথের...

৮৪ বছর পর ওয়েস্ট ইন্ডিজের কোনো বোলার টেস্ট ক্যারিয়ারের প্রথম বলেই উইকেট নিতে পারলেন...

এরপর মার্নাস লাবুশেনের উইকেটও নিয়েছেন তিনি। ও হ্যাঁ, বোলিংয়ের আগে ব্যাটিংয়েও ঝলক দেখিয়েছেন টেস্ট অভিষেকে। ১১ নম্বরে নেমে ৪১ বলে ৩৬ রান করেছেন। শেষ জুটিতে কিমার রোচের সঙ্গে ৫৫ রানের জুটি গড়েছেন...

সব মিলিয়ে, টেস্ট ক্রিকেটের প্রথম দিনটি স্বপ্নময় কেটেছে তার...

দারুণ ব্যাপার বটে। তবে আজকের এই দিনটিতে তার আসার গল্প আরও রোমাঞ্চকর ও প্রেরণাদায়ী...

এই তো, বছর দেড়েক আগেও তিনি চাকরি করতেন একটি সিকিউরিটি ফার্মে। তখন নৈশপ্রহরীর কাজ করেছেন, দেহরক্ষী হিসেবে কাজ করেছেন। উপায় ছিল না, ততদিনে বাবা হয়েছেন, পরিবার আছে, সংসার তো চালাতে হবে...!

তাকে বুঝতে হলে আরেকটু পেছনে যাওয়া যেতে পারে। তিনি উঠে এসেছেন বারাকারা নামের এক গ্রাম থেকে। গায়ানার রাজধানী জর্জটাউন থেকে অনেক দূরে বিচ্ছিন্ন এক গ্রাম সেটি। সেখানে যাওয়ার একমাত্র উপায় ২২৫ কিলোমিটার দীর্ঘ নদীপথ। গাছগাছড়ায় ভরা সেই নদীপথ পাড়ি দিতে কখনও কখনও দুই দিনও লেগে যায়...

গ্রামের জনসংখ্যা এখন সাড়ে তিনশ। ২০১৫ সালের আগে সেখানে কোনো স্থানীয় সরকার ছিল না। টেলিফোন ও ইন্টারনেট নেটওয়ার্কের আওতায় এসেছে এই তো কেবল ২০১৮ সালে। এই গ্রামেই তার জন্ম ১৯৯৯ সালের অগাস্টে। বেড়ে ওঠা সেখানেই...

এটি মূলত মেরুনদের গ্রাম। আফ্রিকা থেকে দাস হিসেবে আনা হয়েছিল যাদের, পরে দাসত্বের শেকল ভেঙে যারা নিজেদের আবাস গড়েছিলেন আমেরিকা ও ক্যারিবিয়ান দ্বীপপুঞ্জের নানা প্রান্তে। গায়ানার একমাত্র মেরুন গ্রাম এটি। উনিশ শতকের গোড়ার দিকে মেরুনরা আবাস গড়েছিলেন এখানে...

এই গ্রামে স্বাস্থ্য কেন্দ্র আছে একটি, আছে একটি প্রাথমিক বিদ্যালয়। মাধ্যমিক শিক্ষার কোনো প্রতিষ্ঠান নেই এখনও। গ্রামের মানুষের মূল পেশা কৃষি আর গাছ কাটা। ভালো কোনো চিকিৎসা বা আধুনিকতার খানিকটা ছোঁয়া পেতে হলেও নদীপথে অনেকটা পাড়ি দিয়ে তাদেরকে যেতে হয় নিউ আমস্টারডাম শহরে...

কৃষি কাজ আর ঘরের কাজ ফাঁকি দিয়ে ছেলেবেলা থেকেই ক্রিকেট খেলতেন তিনি। কখনও লেবু, কখনও পেয়ারা দিয়ে বোলিং করতেন। কাঠের টুকরা, গাছের ডাল সবসময় তার সঙ্গে থাকত, কারণ সেসবই ছিল তার ব্যাট...

একসময় টেপ টেনিসে খেলা শুরু করলেন। গ্রামজুড়ে তার ক্রিকেট প্রতিভার কথা ছড়িয়ে পড়ল। আরেকটু বড় হয়ে মাঝেমধ্যে নিউ আমস্টারডাম শহরে খেলতে যেতেন। তখন কোর্টনি ওয়ালশ, কার্টলি অ্যামব্রোসদের গল্প শুনে, ভিডিও ক্লিপ দেখে, তাদের মতো বোলিং করার চেষ্টা করতেন। এভাবেই একসময় সুযোগ পেয়ে যান ওই শহরের ক্লাব টাকবার পার্কে। যে ক্লাব থেকে উঠে এসেছেন ওয়েস্ট ইন্ডিজের এখনকার দলের অলরাউন্ডার রোমারিও শেফার্ড। ক্রিকেট নিয়ে বড় স্বপ্ন ছিল তার বরাবরই। তবে স্বপ্নগুলো সত্যিকারের ভিত্তি পায় এই ক্লাবে যাওয়ার পর...

তার জীবনের মোড় ঘুরে যাওয়ার শুরু ২০২২ সালে নিউ আমস্টারডামে একটি ফাস্ট বোলিং ক্যাম্পে। প্রতিভা বাছাই করতে আসা কিংবদন্তি কার্টলি অ্যামব্রোসের নজর কাড়েন তিনি। আলাদা করে তার কথা বলে যান অ্যামব্রোস। ক্লাবও তখন তার দিকে বিশেষ মনোযোগ দেয়। ক্লাব তাকে জানিয়ে দেয়, ক্রিকেটের পথে চলতে হলে সিকিউরিটি ফার্মের চাকরি ছাড়তেই হবে...

দ্বিধায় পড়ে যান তিনি। পাঁচ ভাই তিন বোনের বিশাল সংসারে বেড়ে উঠেছেন। আর্থিক কষ্ট থাকলেও না খেয়ে থাকতে হয়নি কখনও। মাছ ধরে, কৃষি কাজ করে খেয়েপরে থাকতে পারতেন সবাই। তবে সেটুকুর জন্য নিত্যদিনের খাটুনি করতেই হতো। ক্রিকেটের স্বপ্ন পূরণের তাড়নাতেই গ্রাম ছেড়ে এসে ওই সিকিউরিটি ফার্মের চাকরি করছিলেন আর পাশাপাশি খেলছিলেন। সেই চাকরি ছেড়ে দিলে এক বছর বয়সী ছেলেকে নিয়ে তার সংসার কীভাবে চলবে, এসবই ভাবছিলেন...

সেসময় তার সঙ্গিনী তাকে বলেছিলেন, 'তুমি যদি ক্রিকেটই খেলতে চাও, জাস্ট গো অ্যান্ড ডু ইট। যা করতে ভালোবাসো, সেটিই করো। আমি আছি তোমার পাশে। পাশে থাকা মানে শুধু আর্থিক সমর্থনই নয়, আমি সবসময় আছি তোমার জন্য... তুমি নিজের স্বপ্নের পথে থাকো, প্রেরণা দেওয়ার জন্য আমি আছি..."

এই ভরসাটুকু পাওয়ার পর আর পেছনে ফিরে তাকাতে হয়নি তাকে। ক্লাব ক্রিকেট খেলে দ্রুতই গায়ানার ক্রিকেটের মূল স্রোতে চলে আসেন। গায়ানার দ্বিতীয় একাদশের হয়ে আঞ্চলিক ক্রিকেটে দুটি ম্যাচ খেলেই মূল  দলে জায়গা করে নেন। গত বছরের ফেব্রুয়ারিতে গায়ানার হয়ে প্রথম শ্রেণির ক্রিকেটে অভিষেক হয়ে যায়...

তার গ্রাম বারাকারায় উৎসবের জোয়ার বয়ে যায় সেদিন। ওই গ্রাম থেকে উঠে এসে কেউ গায়ানার হয়ে খেলবে, গ্রামের মানুষের কাছে কল্পনাতীত ছিল তা...

কিন্তু তার স্বপ্নের সীমানা তো ছিল আরও বড়। অভিষেকে তিন উইকেটের পর দ্বিতীয় ম্যাচেই স্বাদ পেয়ে যান প্রথমবার ৫ উইকেটের। মৌসুমে তিনটা ম্যাচ খেলতে পারেন। তার বোলিং দেখে ক্যারিবিয়ান প্রিমিয়ার লিগে তাকে নেট বোলার হিসেবে রাখা হয়। নেটেও নজর কেড়ে তিনি টুর্নামেন্টের শেষ দিকে মূল দলে জায়গা করে নেন...

সিপিএলে দুটি ম্যাচ খেলে উইকেট পাননি। তবে তার প্রতিভা চাপা থাকেনি।  গত নভেম্বরে দক্ষিণ আফ্রিকা সফরের ওয়েস্ট ইন্ডিজ 'এ' দলে তাকে নেওয়া হয়। সেই সফরে দুই ম্যাচে শিকার করেন ১২ উইকেট। এরপরই অস্ট্রেলিয়া সফরের ওয়েস্ট ইন্ডিজ টেস্ট দলে জায়গা পেয়ে যান স্রেফ পাঁচটি প্রথম শ্রেণির ম্যাচ খেলার অভিজ্ঞতায়। প্রস্তুতি ম্যাচে ভালো করার পর আজকে অ্যাডিলেইডে তাকে টেস্ট ক্যাপ পরিয়ে দেন ইয়ান বিশপ। এরপর তো দিনটি আরও স্মরণীয় করে রাখেন ব্যাটে-বলে পারফরম্যান্সে...

এখন তিনি দুই সন্তানের বাবা। সুখী পরিবার। বারাকারায় গোটা গ্রামটাই অবশ্য সবার পরিবার। আজকে খেলা শেষে তিনি বলেছেন, "বারাকারায় নিশ্চিতভাবেই কেউ আজকে রাতে ঘুমায়নি। সবার চোখ ছিল টিভি পর্দায়। আমার জন্য তাদের সাপোর্ট দারুণ। এখান থেকেও তা অনুভব করতে পারছি আমি। ওই গ্রামের প্রথম কেউ ওয়েস্ট ইন্ডিজের হয়ে খেলছে, এই আনন্দই ওদের কাছে অনেক কিছু। আমিও বারাকারার জন্য অনেক কিছু করতে চাই..."

তার গল্প জানার পর খুঁজে দেখলাম, বারাকারা গ্রাম নিয়ে দারুণ কিছু লেখা আর ডকুমেন্টারি আছে। সেসব পড়লে বা দেখলে আরও ভালো করে বোঝা যায়, কোন দুর্গম দুনিয়া থেকে উঠে এসেছেন তিনি...

দেড় বছর আগেও যিনি ছিলেন নাইটগার্ড আর বডিগার্ড, জীবন ছিল প্রবল অনিশ্চয়তার মোড়ে, সেই তিনি আজ ক্রিকেটবিশ্বে তাক লাগিয়ে অ্যাডিলেইড ওভালের সবুজ আঙিনায় ছুটেছেন সাফল্যের খ্যাপাটে দৌড়ে... 

কেমন রূপকথার মতো শোনায় ...!

আদতে পরিশ্রম, প্রতিজ্ঞা, ত্যাগ আর বিন্দু বিন্দু ঘাম দিয়ে তিনি লিখেছেন জীবনের গল্প। কারও কাছে তা প্রেরণার কাব্য, কারও কাছে সংগ্রামের উপাখ্যান, কারও চোখে রূপকথা...

(📷 Associated Press)

2024-01-30, 1:23 p.m. - Nitun Kundu: IMG-20240130-WA0000.jpg (file attached)
2024-01-30, 2:35 p.m. - Wasel Ur Rahman: *VOTING/NOMINEE Eligibility:*

1.	Any member needs to spend at least one year with the club to earn eligibility to vote and run for a position.

2.	Any member who registered for last year or serves a committee position (including advisors) is eligible to vote/run.

3.	Any alumni, who intends to register/serve in the upcoming season is eligible to vote/run.

4.	An alumni who does not intend to register/serve in a committee in the upcoming season can not vote.

5.	Sponsors, donors or volunteers do not get the right to vote by the virtue of sponsoring/donating/volunteering only.

6.	Votings can be done by open ballot, secret ballots or digital ballots. All members must be notified and given at least 24 hours time to cast vote, if requested. Votes can not be casted by proxy or based on common faith.

2024-02-10, 3:34 p.m. - Simon Bhai Tanveer Ahmed: B-team captain's message:


Hey everyone, sorry I couldn't be here today. Here's my feedback for the B-Team.

First, let's look at how we did last year. 

Well, it wasn't great. In the 40 overs, we got relegated to the 4th Division from the 3rd, and in the T20, we ended up in 5th place. I take the blame for that.

But we shouldn't just judge the B-team by wins and losses. For me, the main goal of our B-team is to give every member a chance to play and enjoy the game.

I've tried my best to make sure everyone has a role in the game and gets to show what they can do, creating meaningful and memorable moments for themselves and their families.

To the new leaders, I say: 

let's keep the B-Team as a place for members to bond and grow, and find a balanced way to give everyone a chance to play while still aiming to win game. 

Finally, let's keep growing and having fun together with the B-Team. ‎<This message was edited>

How to register:
 
STEP 1: Visit www.blood.ca and click “Sign In” 
 
STEP 2: Sign into your account, or create an account 
 
STEP 3: Click on “Partners” in the left task bar 
 
STEP 4: Click “Join an existing team” 
 
STEP 5: Begin typing your partner’s name BCAE (BCAE Bangladesh Canada Association Edmonton) and the search results will pop up below. 
 
STEP 6: Select “yes” and then click “join” 
 
STEP 7: Select “View team”  

STEP 8: Scroll to the bottom of your team’s home page, and you will see the group appointments we have reserved (February 24 2024, 2.05 PM to 2.40PM). To book a spot, click “Book” 
 
STEP 9: Select your time slot and complete the booking.
 
Please contact at ec@bcae.ca for any queries. You can also contact following personnel for any questions:
 
1)    Lamia Khan, General Secretary: 5879374775
2)    Syed Md. Anwarul Islam, Sports and Special Events Secretary: 7806556352

2024-02-25, 8:00 p.m. - Faisol Ferdous added ~ 🤫
2024-02-25, 8:23 p.m. - Humayun: 📣📣📣 *Attention Attention Attention*

Hi RBCC Members,

We hope this message finds you all well. As we embark on this new journey we are dedicated to enhancing the structure and operations of our beloved club.
After careful consideration and deliberation, the Executive Committee has put forth a proposal that we believe could greatly benefit our club. However, we understand the importance of seeking the input and consensus from the majority of our respected members.

It's come to our attention that without a strict bylaw in place, certain aspects of our club's operations, particularly the captaincy selection process, have posed challenges. While the previous method of voting for captains had its merits, it also revealed several areas for improvement.

Here are some of the drawbacks we've identified:

1. Lack of defined criteria and responsibilities for Captains and VCs

2. Captains or vice-captains chosen through a voting system sometimes became unavailable during crucial parts of the season, leading to the need for re-elections for the team.

3. Incidents of captains or vice-captains missing practice sessions and important matches have been observed, indicating a lack of commitment.

4. This lack of availability in practice sessions results in not fully understanding all players' strengths and weaknesses, impacting team coordination, player management, and team selection.

5. Automatic inclusion of the captain in the playing eleven without performance evaluation.

6. In the captaincy selection process using a voting system, sometimes the club face challenges, especially when the team is at risk of relegation. Without the flexibility to make crucial decisions like changing the captaincy if required, it becomes difficult for the club to implement improvements during tough times. Having mid-season evaluations could potentially assist the club in such situations by enabling it to make important decisions when necessary.
2024-02-25, 8:24 p.m. - Humayun: Considering these issues, we propose a new approach to captaincy selection. Under this proposal, a captain selection panel comprising the President, two Vice Presidents, the previous year's President, our esteemed advisor Deen Bhai, and our respected coach will be responsible for selecting captains and vice-captains.


Here are the proposed criteria and responsibilities for Captains and VCs:

1. Should have sound understanding of cricket rules and regulations, including EDCL rules

2. Have to select playing XI based on performance and commitment of the players

3. Have to be fully available throughout the season 

4. Have to attend all practice sessions (except injury, family sickness, being out of the city). If you have other commitments leading to miss the practice sessions, then this job is not for you. 

5. Take full accountability for team kit (making it available during the practice sessions and matches).Also make sure all equipments are back to the kit everytime after use.

6. Involvement in club leadership committee for at least a year. This requirement is applicable only for the Captain selection)

7. Should maintain positive relationship with the club club members, executives, and sponsors.

8. Should have vast knowledge on club culture and its history.
2024-02-25, 8:24 p.m. - Humayun: By following this process, we aim to enhance accountability, transparency, and overall club performance. Regular mid-season evaluations will ensure that our leaders remain accountable and contribute effectively to the team's success.

Therefore, we kindly request your attention and thoughtful consideration regarding the proposal at hand. We believe it is crucial that the majority of our members agree that this decision is in the best interest of the club before moving forward.

Your vote matters greatly in shaping the future of our club. We encourage all members to carefully review the proposal and voice their opinions through the voting process. 

*Information regarding voting will be coming soon.*

Warmest Regards,
Humayun Kabir (VP Communication), RBCC Executive Committee 2024.
2024-02-25, 9:25 p.m. - Sharif Bhai: As the recent Captain of RBB, I agree with some of the drawbacks mentioned above.

I also commend the executive team for providing the opportunity for further discussions and voting arrangements.

My personal opinion is that with this process, there is a possibility that Captains will lose their independence and become subject to the executive’s control.

Three out of six members of the captaincy selection or review committees are from the Executives, which is an influential number. So theoretically, they hold the power to hire and fire the captain. As most of our executive members will also be playing members of the team, there is a possibility that they may receive preferential treatment from the captains over other players.

So, I would request considering one or two democratically elected members in the review committee so that there is a check and balance.

2024-03-01, 8:20 p.m. - Humayun: Attention Everyone,

We have an important announcement regarding the upcoming captaincy selection process. As detailed in the proposal shared earlier, we are conducting an election to determine the method of selecting our captains and vice-captains.

The election will take place this Sunday, and the voting will remain open for 24 hours. If you missed the details of the proposal, don't worry, we'll be sharing it again for your reference.

We're delighted to announce that our very own Nazmus Sakib bhai has graciously volunteered to serve as the election officer for this crucial event.

Here's the plan:

    The voting will be posted on Sunday and will remain open for 24 hours.

    By Monday night, the selected process will be announced.

    If the proposal wins, nominations for captains and vice-captains will be accepted on Tuesday and Wednesday.

    The selection committee will then take two days to discuss and talk to the nominees.

    By Saturday, the names of the captains and vice-captains will be published, and they can begin their duties from Sunday Practice.

If the voting option wins:

    Nominations will be accepted on Tuesday and Wednesday.

    Campaigning will take place on Thursday.

    Voting will be held on Friday and will remain open for 24 hours.

    The winners' names will be announced on Saturday.

Our goal is to select the captains and vice-captains as soon as possible, so let's get ready for an exciting week ahead.

Please note that according to the regulations, members in their first season with us will not be eligible to vote. We have many new faces this year, so we wanted to clarify this beforehand.

Thank you all for your attention, and let's make this election process a success!

Best regards,

Humayun Kabir
VP Communications,
RBCC Execs 24.

2024-03-05, 10:05 p.m. - Imran Kabir Toru: Topic: RBCC 2024 Membership Fees 

Greetings, RBCC members,

On behalf of the 2024 executive team, I want to provide some essential information about the membership fees. We are proposing a fee increase of $50 for the 2024 season due to multiple factors, including upcoming EDCL team dues, the increased price of the Kingsman facility, and other ongoing operating expenses. It has come to our attention recently that one of our kit bags is missing entirely, which has led us to a very critical situation. Also, our existing gears are not in a promising condition, given the wear and tear and usage. These circumstances have forced us to buy almost new gear with multiple items.

We are proposing the following suggestions for a smooth season start with better financial stability in the future.

- Regular members must pay a membership fee of $350, while students who do not have funding, such as undergraduate students, must pay a fee of $175. You can send the membership fee via e-transfer to rbcc.executives@gmail.com.

- To be considered for the games, each member is expected to send at least 50% of the amount of $175 within the deadline of March 20th. The remaining balance has to be paid before the season starts.

If you cannot pay the minimum amount right now, please get in touch with an Exec member at your earliest convenience.

Given the number of paying members, we might decide how many teams and leagues we will participate in.

- Lastly, please message me with any questions or concerns.

Thank you,
-Imran
VP Finance

Greetings RBCC New members,

This message is primarily intended for our new players who recently joined RBCC. First and foremost, welcome to RBCC! We are thrilled to have you and look forward to an exciting season together. I would like to take this opportunity to provide
some important information about the membership fees.

- Regular members are required to pay a membership fee of $350. $175 in payment #1 and the remaining before the season starts. while students who do not have funding, such as undergraduate students, are required to pay a fee of $175. You can send the membership fee via e-transfer to rbcc.executives@gmail.com. Feel free to message me for any questions.

VP Finance

We are excited to extend an invitation to you and your club to join us for the E&DCL Banquet Awards Night, which will take place on Friday, April 19th, 2024. This event promises to be an evening filled with celebration, recognition, and camaraderie as we honor the achievements and contributions of our league members throughout the past season.

Here are the event details:

•	Date: Friday, April 19th, 2024

•	Time: 6:30pm

•	Venue: Meridian Banquets, 4820 76 Ave NW, Edmonton, AB T6B 0A5

The banquet awards night will feature a variety of awards recognizing outstanding performance, sportsmanship, leadership, and dedication within our league. It will be an excellent opportunity for us to come together as a community, reflect on our accomplishments, and build connections with fellow league members.

We cordially invite you to join us for this special occasion. Your presence will contribute to making the evening truly memorable.

Please make sure to buy extra tickets before the event date as tickets will not be available for pickup at the location. Please RSVP by April 15th, 2024 to confirm your attendance and indicate the number of guests joining from your club so that we can reserve the table for you accordingly.

If you have any questions or require further information, please don't hesitate to contact us.

Thank you for your continued support and participation in our league. We eagerly anticipate your presence at the banquet awards night.

Thanks and regards,
Parminder Dhami
Secretary

2024-04-29, 12:40 p.m. - Adnan Bhai: Personal Training: Items that every player should complete every week starting the week of April 29th. The individual will be responsible for completing them. This won't be monitored. I think to grow as a club we need do things to improve our fitness and skills. I came up with list of items. These items will be outside our regular practices and game day. 

The items are as follows:

- 150 catches, high catches, close catches,etc. (just need 2 ppl minimum to do it)
- 100 throws at a stump or wall (can be done by yourself)
- 3 rounds, soccer, or cricket field. (Can be done by yourself)

You can complete them all in one day or get them done in a week.

P.s. Drop a pic when you are doing it on the group

Greetings, RBCC members,

On behalf of the 2024 executive team, I would like to express our gratitude to our president and other executive members for their tireless efforts in obtaining new sponsors and donations over the last couple of months. We are happy to announce that we are keeping the membership fee at $300. A very big thank you to the members who have paid the fee in full or in part. We will refund the extra $50 to the members who have paid $350 in full. 
Since the season has started in full swing, we request that all members pay their remaining outstanding balance of $125 as soon as possible. This will ensure we maintain a healthy financial strength in our fantastic club.

Regards
VP Finance
RBCC Executive Committee 2024

1. When we go to field, we play to win. Our jerseyes do not show A team, B team, good player, bad player etc. It is RBCC. So we play like tigers and support each other.
2. ⁠As the club has A team and B team, purpose of B team would also be personal developement, showcase of talents and attitude.
3. ⁠In B team, we need to fight for each other, and also compete with each other to excel. You get a chance in B team 40, you fight for a spot in B team T20. You get a chance in T20, you fight for a spot in A team. Even if you do not see yourself fighting for spots in upper team, the least would be to beat your own self of yesterday.
4. ⁠Attend practice and show that you are trying to excel. You will get better skilled, work on yesterday's mistakes, learn from veterans. Being casual in attending practice means you prioritize other things (which is understandable for recreational games). But again, that would reflect in team selctions, because it will be unfair to those who have prioritized cricket and trying to improve.
5. ⁠When you reach to the level of A team, you will play a lot in A team, but that might mean less chances in B. As you can see, B team has more players to give chances to. So, team will select the A-level players during high stake games. Do not get disappointed if you have transcended in skills. Remember, through B team, players like you developed, and new players would like to have similar opportunity as well.
2024-05-31, 3:18 p.m. - Rashif: Excellent, do we have a new B team captain?
2024-05-31, 3:19 p.m. - Faisol Ferdous: No, Not yet. Interim Captain

As a former selector and captain, I can tell you that there are lots of things that captains consider when selecting the best XI. There are always exceptions, odd, exceptional, and extenuating circumstances. Only the captains know what is going on in each best XI selection.

But I can tell you one thing: if you see many 'unknown' statuses in your practice sessions, it never gives the captain confidence and also looks bad when he compares you with others.

2024-07-15, 7:41 p.m. - Simon Bhai Tanveer Ahmed: Sitting down and watching a game in Tawa!!!

Ground: From far, it looks nice. Below street level, like Red deer.
Coming down to the field, the pitch is actually lower than the outfield. Outfield is also wavy and uneven. Grass is well cut, but soccer grass. So not the fastest.
Boundary is decent, straight is a bit shorter than side.
Pitch also seems to have fair bounce. Ball came a bit slower (or the batsmen were playing rush shots!!!)

Overall, nothing special about the pitch. But the outfield needed a lot of work to make it even, which was not done.

I saw 3-4 mosquitoes in the hour I stayed here.

2024-07-18, 1:02 p.m. - Simon Bhai Tanveer Ahmed: Gathering in Solidarity with Bangladeshi Protesters at Legislature Ground from 5:30 PM to 6:30 PM today, organized by Bangladeshi Student Association (BSAUA)

The students, community members, and well-wishers of Bangladesh in Edmonton are organizing a gathering in solidarity with the protesters advocating for a fair, just, and meritocratic system in Bangladesh. The protest we support has been, and continues to be, non-political and peaceful. However, counter-protesters have resorted to violence, resulting in the tragic loss of over 60 innocent lives, including minors, and injuring hundreds more.

Moreover, media and online communication in Bangladesh are heavily restricted, preventing the widespread dissemination of this tragedy.

Join us this evening from 5:30 PM to 6:30 PM at the Legislature Grounds. If you support a fair and merit-based system, come and show your support. Regardless of who you are and what you believe, let us stand together for justice, fairness, and equality.

This is not a BSAUA-only event, and individuals from all backgrounds are welcome to join. Feel free to bring placards or banners or whatever with peaceful messages, if you wish.

See you there! 

#StudentLivesMatter

#FairRecruitment

#peacefulprotests

#bangladeshistudents

06 । কারো কাছে যেন ভুল বার্তা না যায় সেদিকে খেয়াল রাখুন।
07। আপনার সন্তান, ভাই, আত্মীয়স্বজন, বন্ধুবান্ধব, পরিচিতদের জানান যে আপনি তাদের যত্ন নিন এবং খুব কমই সোশ্যাল সাইট চালান।
08। রাজনীতি বা বর্তমান বিষয় নিয়ে সরকার বা প্রধানমন্ত্রীর সামনে আপনার কোনো অসংলগ্ন পোস্ট বা ভিডিও...ইত্যাদি প্রেরণ করবেন না। 
09। বর্তমানে কোনো রাজনৈতিক বা ধর্মীয় বিষয়ে বার্তা লেখা বা পাঠানো অপরাধ...এটি করলে বিনা পরোয়ানায় গ্রেপ্তার হতে পারে।
10 । পুলিশ বিজ্ঞপ্তি জারি করবে... এর পর সাইবার ক্রাইম... এর পর অ্যাকশন হবে এটা খুবই গুরুতর।
11 । অনুগ্রহ করে আপনারা সবাই, গ্রুপের সদস্য, এডমিন,... অনুগ্রহ করে বিষয়টি বিবেচনা করুন।
12। ভুল বার্তা যাতে না যায় সেদিকে খেয়াল রাখুন এবং সবাইকে জানিয়ে এই বিষয়টি খেয়াল রাখবেন।
13 । এটি শেয়ার করুন অন‍্যের উপকারে আসবে।

রাজনৈতিক দলগুলোকে আরো সচেতন ও সতর্ক হতে হবে।

 হোয়াটসঅ্যাপ সম্পর্কে গ্রুপ সদস্যদের জন্য গুরুত্বপূর্ণ তথ্য...
👇👇👇
তথ্য ✔ WhatsApp এ
1 | ✔= বার্তা পাঠানো হয়েছে
2 | ✔✔= বার্তা পৌঁছেছে
3 | দুটি নীল ✔✔= বার্তা পড়ুন
4 | তিনটি নীল ✔✔✔= সরকার বার্তা নোট করেছে
5 | দুটি নীল এবং একটি লাল ✔✔✔= সরকার আপনার বিরুদ্ধে ব্যবস্থা নিতে পারে
6 | একটি নীল এবং দুটি লাল = সরকার আপনার তথ্য পরীক্ষা করছে
7 | তিনটি লাল ✔✔✔= সরকার আপনার বিরুদ্ধে ব্যবস্থা গ্রহণ শুরু করেছে এবং শীঘ্রই আপনি আদালতের সমন পাবেন।

একজন দায়িত্বশীল নাগরিক হোন এবং আপনার বন্ধুদের সাথে শেয়ার করুন ।

2024-07-27, 11:02 a.m. - +1 (628) 629-4936: Meta, the parent company of platforms like Facebook and WhatsApp, can share user information with government authorities if someone is considered a national security threat. This can include metadata, which encompasses details such as the time, date, and recipients of messages, even if the content itself remains encrypted. 

For instance, WhatsApp, which uses end-to-end encryption for message content, still shares metadata with law enforcement agencies. This metadata can provide significant insights into a person's communication patterns and has been used in criminal investigations. For example, the U.S. government has successfully used WhatsApp metadata to secure convictions, as seen in the case of Natalie Edwards, a former Treasury Department official [oai_citation:1,How Facebook Undermines Privacy Protections for Its 2 Billion WhatsApp Users — ProPublica](https://www.propublica.org/article/how-facebook-undermines-privacy-protections-for-its-2-billion-whatsapp-users) [oai_citation:2,Here's what data the FBI can get from WhatsApp, iMessage, Signal, Telegram, and more](https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/news/2021/12/heres-what-data-the-fbi-can-get-from-whatsapp-imessage-signal-telegram-and-more) [oai_citation:3,We Now Know What Information the FBI Can Obtain from Encrypted Messaging Apps](https://www.justsecurity.org/79549/we-now-know-what-information-the-fbi-can-obtain-from-encrypted-messaging-apps/).

In summary, while the content of encrypted messages on platforms like WhatsApp may not be accessible, the surrounding metadata can be shared with law enforcement, which can be crucial in national security and criminal investigations.

I remember him getting white fish from his former patients from Lac la Biche and giving it out to us. 2024-08-19, 9:17 a.m. - Sudeepta: Hi guys, I want to bring up something about our club that has bothered me for some time now. I very much dislike the habit of many of our members to smoke during our games. Obviously, the people involved are adults and can make their own choices. However, out of respect for the non-smokers, I think it is reasonable to do this away from the team. I also think it delegitimizes us as a competitive sports team if we are smoking during the game.

I ignored this until now. However, I had to speak up when I saw at least 3 people smoking during the game Saturday in close proximity to Yogesh, who is a youth member. Furthermore, his dad was present at this time. I know that my parents would have been highly critical of me playing cricket in that environment, and honestly, if I had a child, I would not let them be a part of this. 

I hope this is taken seriously and that we can make a club wide rule to not allow smoking during games, or at least have the smokers excuse themselves away from the team if they really need to smoke. 

Thanks.

আস্সালামুআলাইকুম, আপনারা সকলেই ফেনী,  নোয়াখালীর প্রাকৃতিক দুর্যোগ সম্পর্কে অবগত আছেন। বিশেষ করে আমাদের থানার খোঁজখবর পাচ্ছি নিয়মিত। আমার মায়ের ভাষ্যমতে উনি এরকম দুর্যোগ আমাদের এলাকায় আর দেখেননি। 
 অনেক পরিবার গৃহহীন হয়ে, তাদের ঘরবাড়ি ছেড়ে অন্যের বাড়িতে উঠেছে। অনেক পরিবার এখনো শেষ  পর্যন্ত দেখার চেষ্টা করতেছে। 
 এলাকাভিত্তিক কিছু সংগঠন তাদের সাধ্যমত চেষ্টা করতেছে সহযোগিতা করার জন্য। ব্যক্তিগতভাবে এবং সামাজিকভাবে তাদের পক্ষ থেকে আমার কাছে সহযোগিতা চাওয়া হয়েছে। 
 আপনারা যে যার সামর্থ্য অনুযায়ী সহযোগিতার হাত বাড়িয়ে দিতে পারেন। ১০০% আপনার অবদান গরিবদের হাতে পৌঁছাবে বলে নিশ্চয়তা প্রদান করা যাচ্ছে ইনশাআল্লাহ। জাজাকাল্লাহু খাইরান। You can etransfer your donation 
nimran201064@gmail.com

2024-08-23, 2:51 p.m. - Atiqul Islam: I found something to share that may be helpful in our game: Our insights on staying relaxed and focused during a competitive cricket game are crucial. The pressure to win and the fear of failure can lead to stress, negatively impacting performance. However, maintaining a calm mind and relaxed body while staying focused is key to performing well under pressure. To enhance this strategy, consider the following:
1.	Mindfulness and Breathing: Practice mindfulness or deep breathing exercises before and during the game. This helps in calming nerves and maintaining focus.
2.	Positive Visualization: Before the game, visualize performing well. This boosts confidence and reduces anxiety.
3.	Team Support: Support teammates on the ground. Creating a positive and encouraging environment helps reduce individual pressure and promotes team spirit.
4.	Adaptability: Use soft hands, avoid predetermined shots, and allow time for the batsman to find rhythm. Being flexible helps in making better decisions.
5.	Short-Term Focus: Focus on the immediate task at hand, like the next ball or shot, instead of the game's outcome. Breaking the game into smaller, manageable tasks reduces pressure.
6.	Routine and Consistency: Stick to pre-shot routines, whether it's a bowler's run-up or a batsman's stance. Consistency in routine provides a sense of control in high-pressure situations.
Fostering a supportive and constructive team environment is essential for maintaining composure and ensuring everyone plays to their potential
2024-08-23, 2:53 p.m. - Sudeepta: these are excellent points and I encourage everyone to try these out
2024-08-23, 2:54 p.m. - Sudeepta: although I prefer to just see ball, hit ball 😎
2024-08-23, 3:32 p.m. - Sudeepta: In all seriousness though, very good suggestions. I do actually practice many of these items myself
2024-08-23, 3:33 p.m. - Sudeepta: lets get pumped for tomorrow, I feel a strong performance coming

2024-08-28, 2:40 p.m. - Anamul: 📣Important Announcement📣

Dear Members,
After careful considerations and following multiple complaints regarding smoking during games, the Executive Committee has decided to implement a new policy, effective immediately.

Smoking is strictly prohibited during games and in the presence of non-smokers. If you need to smoke, please do so away from the team discussions and gatherings.

Failure to comply with this policy will result in a referral to the Disciplinary Committee for review and further disciplinary actions.

Thank you all for your cooperation and understading. 

Regards,
Anamul
VP Admin

2024-09-17, 6:05 p.m. - Rashif: As our cricket season is coming almost to an end, I wanted to take a moment to reflect on our journey and express my heartfelt gratitude to everyone who made this season memorable.

Firstly, a huge thank you to all the players for your dedication, hard work, and sportsmanship. Every match, practice session, and arguing for the best result was a testament to our commitment and love for the game. 

A special mention to our lunch volunteers, whose delicious and energizing meals kept us fueled and focused throughout the long 40-over games. Your and your families effort behind the scenes did not go unnoticed and were greatly appreciated by all of us.

I also want to extend my deepest thanks to our 12th man of each game. Your readiness to step in at a moment's notice, your support on the field, and your positive attitude made a significant impact on the team.

To our friends, and family, thank you for your unwavering support and cheers from the sidelines. You lifted our spirits and motivated us to give our best every single time.

Let's carry forward the lessons learned into the next season. Here's to more runs, wickets, and unforgettable moments.

Well done, team!

Rashif 
Vice Captain - Team A 2024

Dear Team,

We are excited to announce that net practice sessions for the upcoming 2025 season will officially start after a long wait!

🗓 Start Date: Sunday, January 19th
🕢 Time: 7:30 PM – 9:30 PM
📍 Venue: Pitch n Play
📍 Address: 8227 Roper Rd NW, Edmonton, AB T6E 6S4

The sessions will take place every Sunday moving forward.

We apologize for the venue change, as we were initially planning to hold practices at Govind’s facility. However, due to delays with that arrangement, we have decided to move forward with Pitch n Play.

Please note that practice sessions will be paused during Ramadan due to lower participation observed in previous years.

Looking forward to seeing you all. Thank you

Regards,
Anamul

Recently, my wife received a call from someone pretending to be from a WhatsApp group, asking her to send money. 2025-01-19, 1:45 p.m. - Pritam Da: Batting Coordination Plan for the  Month of January Sessions:

1. First come First to Bat. There will be a sign up sheet to set the batting order.

2. Bat in pairs. Run 3 runs to change ends after each round of batting. 

3. At least 4 or 5 rounds of batting for each batter depending on the number of players attending the session. 

4. Three rounds of regular batting. Last 1 or 2 rounds will be target batting. Target of 10 runs in a round, say suppose from 6 balls. Regular 5 in 4 out field with Sweeper Cover, Long off, long on, cow corner as outside field & short third, point, cover, short mid & square leg as inside field. If you get out twice as per the net bowling coordinator during this individual target batting individual round, your batting for that particular round ends & your partner batts the remaining balls. Net Bowling Coordinator should also decide runs scored during this Target batting period. 

5. Reward/consequences for batsman / bowlers to achieve / fail the target is upto @⁨Sudeepta⁩ to decide.

6. On the other net, we will do drills on one side. Like practicing sweep shots with a drop ball, pull shots on full toss, range hitting if we have space. We need to use tennis balls for this. The other end of this net can be for Mousum, Adnan bhai and Sudeepta to use. We can also split up time as needed.

Please let us know your feedback as necessary to make these sessions effective & productive. Thanks. <This message was edited>

2025-03-09, 11:09 p.m. - Sharif Bhai: I sometimes wonder how difficult it is to be the captain of a leisure or semi-professional league. We have a pool of only 15–25 players, and I don’t think that even if we bring in a charismatic captain like Imran Khan or Ranatunga, they will turn into Kane Williamson if the players don’t perform.

2025-03-09, 11:25 p.m. - Mousum: I wonder why a captain even needs a week to decide. If a squad of 15-25 players has been playing together and everyone’s strengths are well known, why not just pick the captain and vice-captain on the spot?

2025-03-09, 11:40 p.m. - Sharif Bhai: Not a bad idea; I guess most of our cricket experiences are just like that. বিকালে এসে ডাক ডাক বেলি—চলো সবাই খেলি।

It wouldn’t be a problem if all 15-25 players could play at the same time. But since that’s not possible, someone has to take responsibility for selecting the playing XI and leaving out 4-14 players, then live with that decision during the game.

For Team A, it’s much easier since the entire pool seems to have only 11-13 people. I could close my eyes and pick 9 players who always play for Team A.

But the real challenge is that everyone there is capable of taking the lead—so someone has to take charge of changing fielding positions after every single ball. Otherwise, there will be 66 field changes every over.

They just need a captain to establish order, and having a long-term captain saves time.

2025-03-10, 1:15 a.m. - Pritam Da: A Captain/VC/Selector brings the Team Together Off the Field with planning, coordination, preparation, & communication so that we perform together on the field. In "A" team, we never played ডাক ডাক বেলী।

Winning /Losing is just the outcome. A Captain focuses on the entire Process.

How intense/in depth that process will be, depends on the individual.

Finally, "A" Team Dynamics is totally different from the B team. So we definitely have a different perspective & thats completely fine. As a Captain, The Last thing I thought/prepared for was the field placing. I knew who is available when & when who is Unavailable for a longer period & our team composition, players roles were communicated probably 2/3 months earlier for most "A" team players so that we all prepared accordingly. For some people it worked, and definitely some people hated me when they were dropped while some people came back strongly to win us promotion during playoff games after being dropped. One size never fits all. Captaining on the Fly is a process that can also work for some people in certain playing standard. 

Anyways, whats done is done & we cant change that. Hopefully we make the best use of what little time we have left before season. I dont want to just survive in this Div. Some of us are preparing accordingly. Definitely it would have been better if we had unified plan under properly communicated leadership.

2025-03-12, 2:02 p.m. - Sharif Bhai: From previous messages. 

*VOTING/NOMINEE Eligibility:*

1.	Any member needs to spend at least one year with the club to earn eligibility to vote and run for a position.

2.	Any member who registered for last year or serves a committee position (including advisors) is eligible to vote/run.

3.	Any alumni, who intends to register/serve in the upcoming season is eligible to vote/run.

4.	An alumni who does not intend to register/serve in a committee in the upcoming season can not vote.

5.	Sponsors, donors or volunteers do not get the right to vote by the virtue of sponsoring/donating/volunteering only.

6.	Votings can be done by open ballot, secret ballots or digital ballots. All members must be notified and given at least 24 hours time to cast vote, if requested. Votes can not be casted by proxy or based on common faith. <This message was edited>
2025-03-12, 2:03 p.m. - Sharif Bhai: I believe you can not vote or run for a position this year. But definitely you can discuss about bylaws. <This message was edited>
2025-03-12, 2:43 p.m. - Sharif Bhai: Answering various questions is important but also time-consuming. This is one reason I stay clear of executive duties. However, I appreciate the members who have taken on these responsibilities—or had to, upon request (অনুরোধে ঢেঁকি গেলা).

We must remember that all the executives here are volunteers; they are not service providers obligated to offer on-demand assistance. They have their own families, businesses, studies, and jobs, and we should respect their time. Beyond answering inquiries, they also dedicate time to planning events, booking gyms and practice sessions, searching for sponsors, attending meetings, and handling purchases like jerseys and other sports equipment.

We are not a corporation or a large public institution responsible for providing services or ensuring members’ rights. We are simply a club of friends—perhaps a bit more organized than your local sports groups, but not much different.

While members may have many questions and want more information, they also have an obligation to conduct a reasonable search of previous records. It would be helpful if we all made an effort to look things up before asking.

I see a lot of enthusiasm, but I get frustrated when it doesn’t translate into policy discussions or participation in elections. For example, there are many questions about C/VC elections and selections, yet we haven’t had a concrete discussion about their pros and cons or why we need one or another. If you want change, please step up and take on responsible positions to help make those changes happen. Contest in the elections, implement changes you like to make. 

Questioning is good, but we also need to allow the executives to stay focused on their work. Writing bylaws and reviews, conducting elections, selecting captains, and preparing for the AGM are immediate priorities that need to be addressed. So, let’s give them the space to do their work without unnecessary interruptions. And let’s not forget—they are also fasting.

2025-03-19, 8:00 a.m. - Imran Kabir Toru: Subject: RBCC 2025 Membership Fees, Season Commencement, and Important Updates

Dear RBCC Members,

The RBCC Executive Committee extends a warm welcome to all members as we commence the 2025 season. We anticipate a successful and productive year for our club.
To facilitate the timely production of team jerseys and the requisite registration for the EDCL, we kindly request that all members remit their annual membership fee of $350.00, or a minimum initial payment of $175.00, by March 31, 2025. 

These membership fees are critical to the funding of essential team expenditures.
Should any member encounter extenuating circumstances that preclude the fulfillment of the minimum payment requirement, we respectfully request that you engage in a confidential discussion with an Executive Committee member to explore suitable alternative arrangements.

Electronic transfers (e-transfers) should be directed to rbcc.executives@gmail.com.

Please note the following essential updates for the 2025 season:

 * Eligibility: Only fully paid and registered players, both new and returning, will be eligible to participate in the upcoming season.

 * Jersey Policy: Due to new sponsorship requirements, all previous season jerseys will be retired. Players will be required to wear the new 2025 season jersey.

We express our sincere gratitude for your prompt attention to this matter and your continued support. We look forward to a prosperous 2025 season.

Sincerely,
VP Finance
RBCC Executive Committee 2025

2025-03-27, 9:21 a.m. - Sumon: **Same as last year**

Here are the proposed criteria and responsibilities for Captains and VCs:

1. Should have sound understanding of cricket rules and regulations, including EDCL rules

2. Have to select playing XI based on performance and commitment of the players

3. Have to be fully available throughout the season 

4. Have to attend all practice sessions (except injury, family sickness, being out of the city). If you have other commitments leading to miss the practice sessions, then this job is not for you. 

5. Take full accountability for team kit (making it available during the practice sessions and matches).Also make sure all equipments are back to the kit every time after use.

6. Involvement in club leadership committee for at least a year. This requirement is applicable only for the Captain selection)

7. Should maintain positive relationship with the club club members, executives, and sponsors.

8. Should have vast knowledge on club culture and its history.
